text.title: Go Concurrency Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying go-concurrency-patterns. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Service spawns one goroutine per incoming request; under load, 50k goroutines drag the scheduler.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied an 'easy parallel' pattern without a pool.
antipattern.fix: Wrap with errgroup.SetLimit(N) or semaphore.Weighted. Choose N based on downstream resource (DB conns, HTTP target). Verify with pprof.

antipattern.symptom: HTTP handler returns to the client but the spawned goroutine continues hitting the DB for another 30 seconds.
antipattern.root-cause: Author omitted ctx parameter; goroutine had no way to learn about cancellation.
antipattern.fix: Add ctx context.Context to the goroutine. Check ctx.Done() in the work loop. Test with cancelled context.

antipattern.symptom: Pipeline goroutine panics with 'send on closed channel' under flush.
antipattern.root-cause: Cleanup goroutine closed the work channel from the receiving side.
antipattern.fix: Move close() to the sender. If multiple senders, use sync.Once or a coordinator goroutine that owns the close.

antipattern.symptom: First worker error is logged; remaining workers continue and write conflicting results.
antipattern.root-cause: Manual `errC := make(chan error)` instead of errgroup; cancellation logic missed.
antipattern.fix: Replace with errgroup.WithContext. Workers return error directly; errgroup cancels the rest.
